title: Temporarily use older versions of Reactant and GPUCompiler

body:
## Summary

- Reinstates the enum bias change from #5318 (reverted in #5449) by reverting the revert
- Re-enables the `sharding_tests` CI job that #5318 had commented out

This PR tests whether the enum bias change passes sharding/Reactant tests.
Sharding tests currently fail with an IFRT rendezvous timeout — the same failure
occurs on the `bool-bias` branch (#5451), which replaces enums with `Bool`.
Since `Bool` is a valid `ReactantPrimitive`, this rules out enum-specific memory
corruption. The issue appears to be related to compiling a single method with
`ifelse` branching (single concrete type) vs two specialized methods (two struct types).

## Test plan

- [ ] Sharding tests pass (the key test — these were disabled in #5318)
- [ ] All other CI tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Reinstate enum bias (#5318) with sharding tests enabled
